stories_covered: Bloom Energy market cap $93B (up 1500% in one year)
Leo Aschenbrenner's largest fund position (16% of fund) in Bloom
Solid-state fuel cells for AI data center power
Energy as the bottleneck for AI infrastructure
Distributed power vs traditional grid infrastructure
Manufacturing scaling from 1 GW to 2+ GW capacity
harry_takes: AI isn't a bubble; it's a hockey stick on a hockey stick—secular, enduring infrastructure demand for power, not speculative pricing
Leo Aschenbrenner's conviction in Bloom is justified; the company solved a 25-year-long technical problem that markets finally need
Bloom's distributed, modular architecture (50kW units vs 500MW turbines) is fundamentally superior for AI workloads that require millisecond ramp-up
Regulation is now the constraint, not technology or capital; permitting and grid interconnection are the bottlenecks preventing faster deployment
harry_boldest_quote: Don't short the US, don't short Silicon Valley. This is the place where revolutions happen.
predictions: K.R.: Power-to-the-edge will transform city planning and population distribution; energy sovereignty will prevent wars as countries stop relying on adversaries for power
Implied: Bloom's $20B backlog will be absorbed; demand will remain insatiable as AI continues exponential expansion
K.R.: Hydrogen battery storage on-site will complete the distributed energy vision by 2030s; solar + storage + Bloom boxes = true energy independence
harry_role: Harry is the interviewer, driving narrative around Leo's conviction, Bloom's competitive moat, and the scale of the energy problem. He validates K.R.'s 25-year bet and uses the interview to surface the thesis: AI creates secular energy demand that only Bloom's technology can satisfy at the required speed and modularity.
notable: This is a featured guest interview (K.R. Shridhar) rather than a traditional roundtable debate; the episode pivots from skepticism (is this a bubble?) to conviction (here's why it's secular). K.R.'s calm, engineering-minded perspective on risk mitigation shapes the entire narrative.
